response.justification: Candidate is a strong full-stack/web software engineer but lacks the core hardware-focused expertise required for a Principal Performance Architect role. There is no evidence of SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator work, deep neural network training/inference optimization, GPU architecture analysis, SIMD/MIMD experience, or hardware-software co-design. The resume centers on web, mobile, and GIS platforms in TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang — orthogonal to the cloud-hardware optimization scope of this role.
strengths: Extensive multi-decade software engineering experience across stacks
Some low-latency, high-throughput backend work (Sociomantic data integration)
C/C++ background from National Instruments LabWindows/CVI work
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling or hardware accelerator experience
No deep learning, GPU, or SIMD/MIMD architecture background
No advanced degree (PhD/MS) and profile is application-layer, not systems/hardware

key_factors.factor: Missing AI accelerator / SoC modeling expertise
No DNN training/inference or GPU architecture experience
Long C/C++ and low-latency systems exposure
